summary: Completed iteration 4 testing of Speechcue SIP Test system. All features verified: (1) hasUsedCalls flag returned by generateTestOtp (2) Subscribe-to-plan messaging when test calls used (3) Main menu SIP test discovery via greeting line and testSip keyboard button (4) All 4 language files updated with testSip button and keyboard placement.

ui_bugs.issue: When OTP verification fails, generic 'Verification failed. Try again.' shown instead of API error with /testsip
ui_bugs.note: Console shows 'body stream already read' error - response body consumed twice. The API returns correct error but frontend catch block overrides it.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Fix PhoneTestPage verifyOtp function to properly display API error message with /testsip reference (currently catch block shows generic error)
critical_code_review_comments: ✅ phone-test-routes.js generateTestOtp (line 140) returns { error: 'limit_reached', hasUsedCalls: totalCalls > 0 }
✅ _index.js /testsip handler (line 3896-3907) shows subscribe-to-plan message when limit reached
✅ _index.js Test SIP Free button handler (line 7084-7098) properly handles limit_reached
✅ _index.js getMainMenuGreeting (line 1450-1451) shows SIP test line for users who haven't tried SIP test
✅ All 4 language files (en, hi, fr, zh) have testSip button defined at correct lines
✅ All 4 language keyboards include testSip button next to cloudPhone in keyboard array

success_rate.frontend: 92% (11/12 UI checks passed - 1 minor issue with error message display)

test_credentials: N/A - OTP required from Telegram bot @Nomadlybot via /testsip command
context_for_next_testing_agent: All iteration 4 features verified: (1) generateTestOtp returns hasUsedCalls flag when limit reached (2) Both /testsip command and Test SIP Free button show subscribe-to-plan message with Cloud Phone direction when user has used test calls (3) Main menu greeting includes SIP test line for users with 0 test calls (4) All 4 lang files have testSip button + keyboard placement. Minor frontend issue: error message from API with /testsip reference not displayed correctly - generic catch block message shown instead. The Telegram bot code is correct but actual bot interaction cannot be tested.
